ci_support_linux_64_.yaml:
c_compiler:
- gcc
c_compiler_version:
- '14'
c_stdlib:
- sysroot
c_stdlib_version:
- '2.17'
channel_sources:
- conda-forge
channel_targets:
- conda-forge main
cxx_compiler:
- gxx
cxx_compiler_version:
- '14'
docker_image:
- quay.io/condaforge/linux-anvil-x86_64:alma9
libffi:
- '3.5'
libiconv:
- '1'
pcre2:
- '10.47'
target_platform:
- linux-64
zip_keys:
- - c_compiler_version
  - cxx_compiler_version
zlib:
- '1'

name: libglib

about.summary: Provides core application building blocks for libraries and applications written in C.
Provides core application building blocks for libraries and applications written in C.

raw_meta_yaml:
schema_version: 1

context:
  version: "2.88.0"
  major_minor: ${{ (version | split("."))[:2] | join(".") }}
  posix: ${{ "m2-" if win else "" }}
  native: ${{ "m2w64-" if win else "" }}
  # Keep in sync with
  # https://gitlab.gnome.org/GNOME/glib/-/blob/main/meson.build?ref_type=heads#L2468
  minimum_python_version: "3.7"

recipe:
  name: glib-split
  version: ${{ version }}

source:
  url: https://download.gnome.org/sources/glib/${{ major_minor }}/glib-${{ version }}.tar.xz
  sha256: 3546251ccbb3744d4bc4eb48354540e1f6200846572bab68e3a2b7b2b64dfd07
  patches:
    # Related to this patch https://bugzilla.gnome.org/show_bug.cgi?id=673135
    # However, it was rejected by upstream. Homebrew decided to keep their own
    # copy ( https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/patches/59e4d32/glib/hardcoded-paths.diff ).
    # We are essentially using that copy, but with some tweaks. Info at the link below.
    #
    # https://github.com/Homebrew/legacy-homebrew/blob/21ce7a56cb3fc40fe93d731cc654c69268796772/Library/Formula/glib.rb#L33-L39
    - patches/0001-Remove-hard-coded-paths.patch
    ## "Fixes" for tests that apply to all platforms:
    - patches/0002-crossplatform-test-tweaks.patch
    - if: ppc64le or aarch64
      then:
        - patches/0003-Attempt-to-mask-out-qemu-failing-tests.patch
    - if: win
      then:
        - patches/0004-Manually-link-with-libiconv-whenever-we-use-libintl..patch
        - patches/0005-Increase-some-test-timeouts.patch
    ## Seems like our CIs are likely incompatible with some dbus tests
    ## https://github.com/conda-forge/glib-feedstock/pull/199#issuecomment-2578085726
    - if: linux64
      then:
        - patches/0006-Skip-flaky-tests-on-linux64-CI.patch

build:
  number: 0

outputs:
  - staging:
      name: glib-build
    requirements:
      ignore_run_exports:
        from_package:
          - python
      build:
        - gettext-tools
        - if: win
          then:
            - m2-filesystem
            - ${{ posix }}patch
        - meson
        - ninja
        - ${{ native }}pkg-config
        - ${{ compiler("c") }}
        - ${{ stdlib("c") }}
        - ${{ compiler("cxx") }}
        # While python is purely a build tool, the name of the python executable
        # seems to appear in some shebang lines in the installed code.
        # We omit the cross-guard here as the build python is also used in g-ir-scanner
        - python >=${{ minimum_python_version }}
        - setuptools
        - if: build_platform != target_platform
          then:
            - cross-python_${{ target_platform }}
            - packaging
            - libffi
            - zlib
            - pcre2
            - libiconv
            - if: not linux
              then:
                - libintl-devel
      host:
        - python >=${{ minimum_python_version }}
        - setuptools
        - libffi
        - zlib
        - pcre2
        - libiconv
        - if: not linux
          then:
            - libintl-devel

  - package:
      name: libglib
    inherit: glib-build
    build:
      script:
        - if: unix
          then:
            - install.sh
          else:
            - install.bat
    requirements:
      ignore_run_exports:
        from_package:
          - python
          - if: unix
            then:
              - ${{ compiler("cxx") }}
      build:
        - gettext-tools
        - if: win
          then:
            - ${{ posix }}patch
        - meson
        - ninja
        - ${{ native }}pkg-config
        - ${{ compiler("c") }}
        - ${{ stdlib("c") }}
        - ${{ compiler("cxx") }}
        - ninja
        - python >=${{ minimum_python_version }}
        - packaging
        - setuptools
      host:
        - libffi
        - if: not linux
          then:
            - libintl-devel
        - zlib
        - pcre2
        - libiconv
      run_exports:
        - ${{ pin_subpackage("libglib") }}
      run_constraints:
        # Avoid colliding with older glib builds that didn"t have a libglib output
        - glib >2.66
    tests:
      - script:
        - if: osx
          then:
            - test -f ${PREFIX}/lib/libglib-2.0.0${SHLIB_EXT}
            - test -f ${PREFIX}/lib/libgirepository-2.0.0${SHLIB_EXT}
        - if: linux
          then:
            - test -f ${PREFIX}/lib/libglib-2.0${SHLIB_EXT}.0
            - test -f ${PREFIX}/lib/libgirepository-2.0${SHLIB_EXT}.0
        - if: win
          then:
            - if not exist %LIBRARY_PREFIX%\share\gir-1.0\GLib-2.0.gir exit 1
            - if not exist %LIBRARY_PREFIX%\lib\girepository-1.0\GLib-2.0.typelib exit 1
            - if not exist %LIBRARY_PREFIX%\lib\girepository-2.0.lib exit 1
            - if not exist %LIBRARY_PREFIX%\lib\pkgconfig\girepository-2.0.pc exit 1
            - if not exist %LIBRARY_PREFIX%\include\glib-2.0\girepository\girepository.h exit 1
          else:
            - test ! -f ${PREFIX}/lib/libgobject-2.0.la
            - test ! -f ${PREFIX}/lib/libglib-2.0${SHLIB_EXT}
            - test -f ${PREFIX}/etc/conda/activate.d/libglib_activate.sh
            - test -f ${PREFIX}/etc/conda/deactivate.d/libglib_deactivate.sh
        - if: unix
          then:
            - test -f ${PREFIX}/lib/pkgconfig/glib-2.0.pc
            - test -f ${PREFIX}/share/gir-1.0/GLib-2.0.gir
            - test -f ${PREFIX}/lib/girepository-1.0/GLib-2.0.typelib
            - test -f ${PREFIX}/lib/pkgconfig/girepository-2.0.pc
            - test -f ${PREFIX}/include/glib-2.0/girepository/girepository.h

  - package:
      name: glib-tools
    inherit:
      from: glib-build
      run_exports: false
    build:
      script:
        - if: unix
          then:
            - install.sh
          else:
            - install.bat
    requirements:
      ignore_run_exports:
        from_package:
          - python *
          - if: unix
            then:
              - ${{ compiler("cxx") }}
          - libglib  # override libglib's loose run_export pin with our specific one
          - libiconv
          - zlib
      build:
        - gettext-tools
        - if: win
          then:
            - ${{ posix }}patch
        - meson
        - ninja
        - ${{ native }}pkg-config
        - ${{ compiler("c") }}
        - ${{ stdlib("c") }}
        - ${{ compiler("cxx") }}
        - ninja
        - python >=${{ minimum_python_version }}
        - packaging
        - setuptools
      host:
        - ${{ pin_subpackage("libglib", exact=True) }}
        - if: not linux
          then:
            - libintl-devel
        - libiconv
        - zlib
      run:
        - ${{ pin_subpackage("libglib", exact=True) }}
        - libffi
    tests:
      - script:
          # Check that binaries can run -- instigated by Meson Linux rpath issue
          - if: linux
            then:
              - gapplication help
          - gdbus help
          - gio version
          - gio-querymodules .
          - glib-compile-schemas --help
          - gresource help
          - gi-compile-repository --help
          - gi-decompile-typelib --help
          - gi-inspect-typelib --help

  - package:
      name: glib
    inherit:
      from: glib-build
      run_exports: false
    build:
      script:
        - if: unix
          then:
            - install.sh
          else:
            - install.bat
    requirements:
      ignore_run_exports:
        from_package:
          - python *
          - if: unix
            then:
              - ${{ compiler("cxx") }}
              - ${{ compiler("c") }}
              - ${{ stdlib("c") }}
          - libiconv
          - pcre2
          - zlib
          - libffi
          - libglib  # override libglib's loose run_export pin with our specific one
          - if: win
            then:
              - m2-sed
        by_name:
          # This is intentional to workaround conda-build"s overdependening checks.
          - libintl-devel
      build:
        - gettext-tools
        - if: win
          then:
            - ${{ posix }}patch
        - meson
        - ninja
        - ${{ native }}pkg-config
        - ${{ compiler("c") }}
        - ${{ stdlib("c") }}
        - ${{ compiler("cxx") }}
        - ninja
        - python >=2.7
        - packaging
        - setuptools
      host:
        - ${{ pin_subpackage("libglib", exact=True) }}
        - ${{ pin_subpackage("glib-tools", exact=True) }}
        - if: not linux
          then:
            - libintl-devel
        - libffi
        - libiconv
        - pcre2
        - zlib
      run:
        - python *
        # The `packaging` Python module is needed by gdbus-codegen. In certain
        # build scenarios, that script is unavoidably executed by a Python
        # interpreter different from the one installed into the "run"
        # environment, so you may need to explicitly add a dep on `packaging`
        # elsewhere. See https://github.com/conda-forge/glib-feedstock/pull/182
        - packaging
        - ${{ pin_subpackage("libglib", exact=True) }}
        - ${{ pin_subpackage("glib-tools", exact=True) }}
        # glib headers depend on libintl headers
        - if: not linux
          then:
            - libintl-devel
      run_exports:
        - ${{ pin_subpackage("libglib") }}
    tests:
      - script:
          - glib-compile-resources --help
          - gobject-query --help
          - if: unix
            then:
              - test -f ${PREFIX}/lib/libglib-2.0${SHLIB_EXT}
              # hmaarrfk - 2024/10 -- The PREFIX may end up becoming "/usr/bin/env python"
              # due to the way that conda handles very long shebang lines
              # https://github.com/conda-forge/glib-feedstock/pull/192#discussion_r1786546999
              - head -n 1 ${PREFIX}/bin/glib-mkenums | grep -E "^.!(${PREFIX}|/usr/bin/env python)"
              - head -n 1 ${PREFIX}/bin/glib-genmarshal | grep -E "^.!(${PREFIX}|/usr/bin/env python)"
              - head -n 1 ${PREFIX}/bin/gtester-report | grep -E "^.!(${PREFIX}|/usr/bin/env python)"
              - gtester --help
              - gdbus-codegen --help
              - ${CC} ${LDFLAGS} ${CFLAGS} test.c $(pkg-config --cflags --libs glib-2.0) -o test-glib && ./test-glib
          - if: win
            then:
              - test-win.bat
        requirements:
          run:
            - ${{ compiler("c") }}
            - pkg-config
        files:
          recipe:
            - test.c
            - test-win.bat

about:
  homepage: https://developer.gnome.org/glib/
  repository: https://gitlab.gnome.org/GNOME/glib/
  license: LGPL-2.1-or-later
  license_file: LICENSES/LGPL-2.1-or-later.txt
  summary: Provides core application building blocks for libraries and applications written in C.

extra:
  feedstock-name: glib
  recipe-maintainers:
    - ccordoba12
    - jakirkham
    - scopatz
    - ocefpaf
    - pkgw
    - isuruf
    - xhochy
    - tschoonj

about.summary: Provides core application building blocks for libraries and applications written in C.
